logline.en: I see Julius Caesar in Rome. He is a bold leader, a general, and a dreamer. People cheer for him in the streets. Caesar is not only powerful—he changes Rome forever with his ideas, his victories, and with his end. Even today, people use his name for power—"Caesar" and "Kaiser."
logline.pt: Sometimes I close my eyes and try to imagine the ancient world, full of famous names and brave hearts. Between 100 BCE and 400 CE, so many people left a mark, but six stand out. When I follow their lives, I see how one person can change everything for millions.

logline.en: Far to the east, in India, I meet Ashoka the Great. At first, he is a fierce king. After a big battle, he feels pain and sorrow, so he chooses peace. Ashoka spreads the message of Buddhism, respect, and understanding. Stones around his empire carry his words.
logline.pt: In a small land under Roman rule, a boy grows up with hope and kindness in his heart. His name is Jesus. He speaks with simple words. He teaches about love, forgiveness, and helping others. Many people listen, many are afraid. In the end, his words cross the world. Centuries pass, and billions follow his path. Jesus is the most influential person in history—his message of love still lives every day.

logline.en: Then I arrive in Judea and meet Paul the Apostle. He never met Jesus in person, but his life changes on a road. Paul travels far—he speaks in big cities, tells the story of Jesus to many people. He writes letters, starts churches, and his words help spread Christianity across the world.
logline.pt: In China, I hear the name Han Wudi, the "Martial Emperor." He believes deeply in learning. He opens schools and brings China together, creating the great Han dynasty. His time is long and strong. Even today, the people of China call themselves "Han people."

logline.en: These six—Caesar, Jesus, Ashoka, Han Wudi, Paul, Augustus—were not perfect, but each gave something powerful: new ideas, new ways to rule, new hope, or new peace. They lived, they spoke, they acted—and our world still remembers. Their stories remind me: one life, with courage and heart, can change everything.
logline.pt: Finally, in Rome, I see Augustus, the first emperor. He starts a new time—no more endless battles inside Rome. He builds great buildings and roads, and gives peace a new name: "Pax Romana." Under Augustus, Rome is the heart of the world.

prompts.cover: Meet six legendary people—each one a story, each one a turning point for the world.
